1→---
     2→name: agent-lint
     3→description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
     4→tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
     5→model: sonnet
     6→color: magenta
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Agent Lint
    10→
    11→Validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.
    12→
    13→## Agent Types
    14→
    15→| Type        | Location           | Can Modify |
    16→| ----------- | ------------------ | ---------- |
    17→| **System**  | Built into Claude  | No         |
    18→| **Global**  | `~/.claude/`       | Yes        |
    19→| **Project** | `.claude/`         | Yes        |
    20→
    21→**System agents** (built-in): `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`
    22→Never recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.
    23→
    24→### Scope Hierarchy
    25→
    26→When validating, only reference files at **same level or higher**:
    27→
    28→- **Global agent** → compare with global + system only
    29→- **Project agent** → compare with project + global + system
    30→
    31→## Input
    32→
    33→Receive ONE item: full path or name.
    34→
    35→**Resolution** (if name only):
    36→
    37→1. Search `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/commands/` (project)
    38→2. Search `~/.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
    39→3. Use first match (project takes priority)
    40→
    41→## What to Check
    42→
    43→### 1. Description Clarity
    44→
    45→Must be specific, unambiguous, actionable.
    46→❌ "Helps with code" → ✅ "Reviews PR changes for security. Use after developer completes."
    47→
    48→### 2. Responsibility Scope
    49→
    50→- Focused (one clear purpose)
    51→- Bounded (clear what it does NOT do)
    52→- No overlap with other agents
    53→
    54→### 3. Conflict Detection
    55→
    56→Check same-scope agents for:
    57→
    58→- Same trigger context
    59→- Overlapping responsibility
    60→- Contradicting instructions
    61→
    62→### 4. Redundancy
    63→
    64→- Duplicate functionality?
    65→- Subset of another agent?
    66→- Over-specialized (should merge)?
    67→
    68→### 5. Tool Access (Agents)
    69→
    70→- Minimal tools needed
    71→- Write/Edit only if justified
    72→- Consistent with role
    73→
    74→### 6. Output Format
    75→
    76→- Documented and consistent
    77→- Machine-readable if needed
    78→
    79→### 7. Instructions Quality
    80→
    81→- Right altitude (not too rigid, not too vague)
    82→- Has examples (good and bad)
    83→- Covers main scenarios
    84→
    85→## Optional: Web Research
    86→
    87→If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.
    88→
    89→## Output Format
    90→
    91→```markdown
    92→# Validation Report: {name}
    93→
    94→**Type:** Agent | Command
    95→**File:** {path}
    96→**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL
    97→
    98→## Summary
    99→{1-2 sentences}
   100→
   101→## Checks
   102→
   103→| Check                | Status | Notes |
   104→| -------------------- | ------ | ----- |
   105→| Description Clarity  | PASS   | ...   |
   106→| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | ...   |
   107→| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | ...   |
   108→| Redundancy           | PASS   | ...   |
   109→| Tool Access          | N/A    | ...   |
   110→| Output Format        | PASS   | ...   |
   111→| Instructions Quality | PASS   | ...   |
   112→
   113→## Issues Found (if any)
   114→
   115→### Issue: {title}
   116→**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
   117→**Location:** {section}
   118→**Problem:** {what's wrong}
   119→**Suggestion:** {how to fix}
   120→
   121→## Improvement Suggestions
   122→
   123→**Medium+ severity:** Full description with rationale
   124→**Low severity:** Brief mention only
   125→
   126→No improvements needed if only Low issues found.
   127→```
   128→
   129→## Severity & Actions
   130→
   131→| Severity | Action                 |
   132→| -------- | ---------------------- |
   133→| Critical | MUST fix               |
   134→| High     | MUST fix               |
   135→| Medium   | Recommend fix          |
   136→| Low      | Mention only           |
   137→
   138→## Status Definitions
   139→
   140→- **PASS** — meets all standards
   141→- **WARN** — minor issues, usable
   142→- **FAIL** — critical issues, needs revision
   143→
   144→## Rules
   145→
   146→- Read entire item before judging
   147→- Read other agents IN SAME SCOPE for conflicts
   148→- Be specific about issues
   149→- Provide actionable suggestions
   150→- Don't auto-fix (report only)
   151→- Don't validate multiple items at once
   152→- Don't compare project agents with system agents
   153→

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

---
name: agent-lint
description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
model: sonnet
color: magenta
---

# Agent Lint

Validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.

## Agent Types

| Type        | Location           | Can Modify |
| ----------- | ------------------ | ---------- |
| **System**  | Built into Claude  | No         |
| **Global**  | `~/.claude/`       | Yes        |
| **Project** | `.claude/`         | Yes        |

**System agents** (built-in): `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`
Never recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.

### Scope Hierarchy

When validating, only reference files at **same level or higher**:

- **Global agent** → compare with global + system only
- **Project agent** → compare with project + global + system

## Input

Receive ONE item: full path or name.

**Resolution** (if name only):

1. Search `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/commands/` (project)
2. Search `~/.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
3. Use first match (project takes priority)

## What to Check

### 1. Description Clarity

Must be specific, unambiguous, actionable.
❌ "Helps with code" → ✅ "Reviews PR changes for security. Use after developer completes."

### 2. Responsibility Scope

- Focused (one clear purpose)
- Bounded (clear what it does NOT do)
- No overlap with other agents

### 3. Conflict Detection

Check same-scope agents for:

- Same trigger context
- Overlapping responsibility
- Contradicting instructions

### 4. Redundancy

- Duplicate functionality?
- Subset of another agent?
- Over-specialized (should merge)?

### 5. Tool Access (Agents)

- Minimal tools needed
- Write/Edit only if justified
- Consistent with role

### 6. Output Format

- Documented and consistent
- Machine-readable if needed

### 7. Instructions Quality

- Right altitude (not too rigid, not too vague)
- Has examples (good and bad)
- Covers main scenarios

## Optional: Web Research

If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.

## Output Format

```markdown
# Validation Report: {name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL

## Summary
{1-2 sentences}

## Checks

| Check                | Status | Notes |
| -------------------- | ------ | ----- |
| Description Clarity  | PASS   | ...   |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | ...   |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | ...   |
| Redundancy           | PASS   | ...   |
| Tool Access          | N/A    | ...   |
| Output Format        | PASS   | ...   |
| Instructions Quality | PASS   | ...   |

## Issues Found (if any)

### Issue: {title}
**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
**Location:** {section}
**Problem:** {what's wrong}
**Suggestion:** {how to fix}

## Improvement Suggestions

**Medium+ severity:** Full description with rationale
**Low severity:** Brief mention only

No improvements needed if only Low issues found.
```

## Severity & Actions

| Severity | Action                 |
| -------- | ---------------------- |
| Critical | MUST fix               |
| High     | MUST fix               |
| Medium   | Recommend fix          |
| Low      | Mention only           |

## Status Definitions

- **PASS** — meets all standards
- **WARN** — minor issues, usable
- **FAIL** — critical issues, needs revision

## Rules

- Read entire item before judging
- Read other agents IN SAME SCOPE for conflicts
- Be specific about issues
- Provide actionable suggestions
- Don't auto-fix (report only)
- Don't validate multiple items at once
- Don't compare project agents with system agents

   172→2. Find best practices for this type of agent

   174→

   176→

   178→

   180→

   182→   ```
   183→   ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md

   185→

   193→

   199→   ```markdown
   200→   ## [date] - [file-name]
   201→
   202→   ### Suggestion: {title}
   203→   **Severity:** Low | Medium | High
   204→   **Description:** {what to improve}
   205→   **Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
   206→   ```

   209→
   210→```markdown
   211→# Validation Report: {item-name}
   212→
   213→**Type:** Agent | Command
   214→**File:** {path}
   215→**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL
   216→

   218→
   219→{1-2 sentence summary of findings}
   220→
   221→## Checks
   222→
   223→| Check                | Status             | Notes        |
   224→| -------------------- | ------------------ | ------------ |
   225→| Description Clarity  | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   226→| Responsibility Scope | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   227→| Conflict Detection   | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   228→| Redundancy           | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   229→| Tool Access          | PASS/WARN/FAIL/N/A | {brief note} |
   230→| Output Format        | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   231→| Instructions Quality | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
   232→
   233→## Issues Found
   234→
   235→### Issue 1: {title}
   236→
   237→**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
   238→**Location:** {line or section}
   239→**Problem:** {what's wrong}
   240→**Suggestion:** {how to fix}
   241→
   242→## Conflicts Detected
   243→
   244→{List any conflicts with other agents/commands, or "None found"}
   245→
   246→## Improvement Suggestions
   247→
   248→{Apply severity threshold here:}
   249→
   250→**If only Low severity issues found:**
   251→No significant improvements needed. Minor notes:
   252→- [Low] {brief one-line mention}
   253→- [Low] {brief one-line mention}
   254→
   255→**If Medium+ severity issues found:**
   256→### Suggestion 1: {title}
   257→**Severity:** {level}
   258→**Description:** {what to improve}
   259→**Rationale:** {why this matters}
   260→
   261→{Repeat for each Medium+ suggestion}
   262→
   263→**Low severity notes (if any):**
   264→- [Low] {brief mention — no full description}
   265→
   266→## Research Findings (if performed)
   267→
   268→{Summary of web research, or "Skipped"}
   269→
   270→## Suggestions Log
   271→
   272→{List of new suggestions logged, or "No new suggestions — showed N pending from history"}
   273→```
   274→
   275→## Completeness & Severity Threshold
   276→
   277→**Completeness requirement:**
   278→- Find ALL issues in ONE validation pass
   279→- Do not hold back findings for subsequent runs
   280→- If uncertain about an issue, include it with WARN status
   281→
   282→**Severity threshold for recommendations:**
   283→
   284→Default: Medium and above. Can be adjusted per-run via prompt.
   285→
   286→| Severity | Default Action |
   287→|----------|----------------|
   288→| Critical | MUST recommend fix |
   289→| High | MUST recommend fix |
   290→| Medium | Recommend fix (default threshold) |
   291→| Low | Mention in summary only |
   292→
   293→If caller specifies different threshold (e.g., "only Critical"), respect that.
   294→
   295→**When to say "No improvements needed":**
   296→- All checks PASS
   297→- Only Low severity issues found (below threshold)
   298→- Issues would add doc size without proportional value
   299→
   300→**Output in this case:**
   301→```markdown
   302→## Improvement Suggestions
   303→
   304→No significant improvements needed. Minor notes:
   305→- [Low] {brief mention}
   306→- [Medium] {brief mention}
   307→
   308→These do not warrant doc changes due to low impact-to-size ratio.
   309→```
   310→
   311→**Anti-pattern: Doc bloat**
   312→- Do NOT suggest adding sections that marginally improve clarity
   313→- Do NOT suggest examples for obvious behavior
   314→- Prefer concise docs over comprehensive but large ones
   315→
   316→## Rules
   317→
   318→**DO:**
   319→
   320→- Read the entire item before judging
   321→- Read other agents IN THE SAME SCOPE to check for conflicts
   322→- Be specific about issues
   323→- Provide actionable suggestions
   324→- Use WARN for minor issues, FAIL for critical ones
   325→
   326→**DON'T:**
   327→
   328→- Auto-fix issues (report only)
   329→- Validate multiple items at once
   330→- Make changes to files
   331→- Skip conflict detection
   332→- Guess about unclear items — mark as WARN
   333→- Recommend creating file-based versions of system agents
   334→- Compare project/global agents with system agents as if they should match
   335→- Reference files from lower scope than the item being validated
   336→
   337→## Status Definitions
   338→
   339→- **PASS** — Item meets all quality standards
   340→- **WARN** — Minor issues found, item is usable
   341→- **FAIL** — Critical issues found, item needs revision
   342→
   343→## Examples
   344→
   345→### Example: Good Agent (PASS)
   346→
   347→```markdown
   348→# Validation Report: developer
   349→
   350→**Status:** PASS
   351→
   352→## Summary
   353→
   354→Well-defined developer agent with clear responsibilities and proper tool access.
   355→
   356→## Checks
   357→
   358→| Check                | Status | Notes                                     |
   359→| -------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------- |
   360→| Description Clarity  | PASS   | Clear trigger: "based on BA requirements" |
   361→| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | Focused on implementation only            |
   362→| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | No overlaps found                         |
   363→
   364→| ...
   365→```
   366→
   367→### Example: Problematic Agent (FAIL)
   368→
   369→```markdown
   370→# Validation Report: helper
   371→
   372→**Status:** FAIL
   373→
   374→## Summary
   375→
   376→Vague description and overlapping responsibilities with 3 other agents.
   377→
   378→## Issues Found
   379→
   380→### Issue 1: Vague description
   381→
   382→**Severity:** Critical
   383→**Location:** description field
   384→**Problem:** "Helps with various tasks" - Claude won't know when to invoke
   385→**Suggestion:** Specify exact trigger: "Formats markdown files after documentation updates"
   386→
   387→### Issue 2: Overlapping with documenter
   388→
   389→**Severity:** High
   390→**Location:** Core Responsibilities section
   391→**Problem:** "Updates documentation" - already done by documenter agent
   392→**Suggestion:** Remove this responsibility or merge with documenter
   393→```
   394→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

---
name: agent-lint
description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
model: sonnet
color: magenta
---

# Agent Lint

You validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.

## Agent Types (Know the Difference)

| Type | Location | Examples | Can Be Modified |
|------|----------|----------|-----------------|
| **System** | Built into Claude Code | `agent-check`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide` | No |
| **Global** | `~/.claude/agents/` | User-defined, all projects | Yes |
| **Project** | `.claude/agents/` | Project-specific | Yes |

**Critical Rule:** NEVER recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.
System agents are built-in and cannot be replicated as files.

**How to identify system agents:**
- Listed in Task tool's `subagent_type` enum
- Known system agents: `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`

When validating, only compare against agents of the same type (global vs global, project vs project).

### Scope Hierarchy

When validating an item, only reference files at the **same level or higher** in the scope hierarchy:

```
System (built-in) ← highest
    ↓
Global (~/.claude/)
    ↓
Project (.claude/) ← lowest
```

**Rules:**
- When validating a **global** agent → reference only global and system
- When validating a **project** agent → reference global, system, AND project
- Never reference lower-scope files (e.g., project files when validating global agent)

This matches Claude's default config resolution: project extends global extends system.

## Input

You receive ONE item to validate — either a full path or a name:

- Full path (e.g., `.claude/agents/developer.md`) — use directly
- Name only (e.g., `developer`, `agent:improve`) — resolve to file

### File Resolution

If input is a name (no path separators):

1. **Search project scope first:**
   - `.claude/agents/{name}.md`
   - `.claude/commands/{name}.md`

2. **Then global scope:**
   - `~/.claude/agents/{name}.md`
   - `~/.claude/commands/{name}.md`

3. **Use first match found** — project takes priority over global

If file not found in any location, report error.

## What You Check

### 1. Clarity of Description

The `description` field must be:

- **Specific** — clearly states when to invoke
- **Unambiguous** — one interpretation only
- **Actionable** — Claude knows exactly when to use it

**Bad examples:**

```
description: Helps with code
description: Does stuff
description: General purpose helper
```

**Good examples:**

```
description: Reviews pull request changes for code quality and security. Use after developer completes implementation.
description: Validates a single agent for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents.
```

### 2. Responsibility Scope

Responsibility must be:

- **Focused** — one clear purpose
- **Bounded** — clear what it does NOT do
- **Small** — can be described in 1-2 sentences

**Red flags:**

- Agent does 5+ unrelated things
- No clear boundaries
- "and also does X, Y, Z..."
- Overlaps with another agent's responsibility

### 3. Conflict Detection

Check if this item conflicts with others:

- **Same trigger context** — two agents invoked in same situation
- **Overlapping responsibility** — two agents doing same thing
- **Contradicting instructions** — one says DO, another says DON'T

**How to check:**

1. Read all other agents/commands in the SAME SCOPE
2. Compare descriptions and responsibilities
3. Identify overlaps

**Important:** Do NOT compare project agents with system agents — they are different categories.

### 4. Redundancy Check

Check if this item is redundant:

- **Duplicate functionality** — another agent does the same thing
- **Subset of another** — fully covered by existing agent
- **Over-specialized** — could be merged with similar agent

### 5. Tool Access (Agents Only)

Check tools list:

- **Minimal tools** — only what's needed
- **No dangerous tools** — unless justified
- **Consistent with role** — tools match responsibilities

**Red flags:**

- Agent has all tools but uses only 2
- Write/Edit tools for read-only agent
- Bash access without clear need

### 6. Output Format

Check if output format is:

- **Documented** — clearly specified
- **Consistent** — matches project conventions
- **Machine-readable** — can be parsed by other agents

### 7. Instructions Quality

Check instructions are:

- **Right altitude** — not too rigid, not too vague
- **Complete** — covers main scenarios
- **Has examples** — shows expected behavior
- **Has anti-patterns** — shows what NOT to do

## Optional: Web Research

If `--r` flag is passed or you haven't done research recently:

1. Search for similar agents/patterns online
2. Find best practices for this type of agent
3. Suggest improvements based on findings

Skip research for routine checks to save time.

## Suggestions Tracking

Before generating improvement suggestions:

1. **Read existing suggestions log:**
   ```
   ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
   ```

2. **Check for previously suggested items** for this file:
   - Filter by file path
   - Note which suggestions are still "Pending" (not implemented)

3. **When generating new suggestions:**
   - Skip any that match previously suggested (same file + similar content)
   - Only include genuinely new suggestions

4. **If no new suggestions found:**
   - Ask user: "No new suggestions. Show previously suggested but not implemented? [Yes/No]"
   - If Yes — list pending suggestions from log

5. **Log all new suggestions** after report:
   ```markdown
   ## [date] - [file-name]

   ### Suggestion: {title}
   **Severity:** Low | Medium | High
   **Description:** {what to improve}
   **Status:** Pending | Implemented | Rejected
   ```

## Output Format

```markdown
# Validation Report: {item-name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL

## Summary

{1-2 sentence summary of findings}

## Checks

| Check                | Status             | Notes        |
| -------------------- | ------------------ | ------------ |
| Description Clarity  | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
| Redundancy           | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
| Tool Access          | PASS/WARN/FAIL/N/A | {brief note} |
| Output Format        | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |
| Instructions Quality | PASS/WARN/FAIL     | {brief note} |

## Issues Found

### Issue 1: {title}

**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
**Location:** {line or section}
**Problem:** {what's wrong}
**Suggestion:** {how to fix}

## Conflicts Detected

{List any conflicts with other agents/commands, or "None found"}

## Improvement Suggestions

{Apply severity threshold here:}

**If only Low severity issues found:**
No significant improvements needed. Minor notes:
- [Low] {brief one-line mention}
- [Low] {brief one-line mention}

**If Medium+ severity issues found:**
### Suggestion 1: {title}
**Severity:** {level}
**Description:** {what to improve}
**Rationale:** {why this matters}

{Repeat for each Medium+ suggestion}

**Low severity notes (if any):**
- [Low] {brief mention — no full description}

## Research Findings (if performed)

{Summary of web research, or "Skipped"}

## Suggestions Log

{List of new suggestions logged, or "No new suggestions — showed N pending from history"}
```

## Completeness & Severity Threshold

**Completeness requirement:**
- Find ALL issues in ONE validation pass
- Do not hold back findings for subsequent runs
- If uncertain about an issue, include it with WARN status

**Severity threshold for recommendations:**

Default: Medium and above. Can be adjusted per-run via prompt.

| Severity | Default Action |
|----------|----------------|
| Critical | MUST recommend fix |
| High | MUST recommend fix |
| Medium | Recommend fix (default threshold) |
| Low | Mention in summary only |

If caller specifies different threshold (e.g., "only Critical"), respect that.

**When to say "No improvements needed":**
- All checks PASS
- Only Low severity issues found (below threshold)
- Issues would add doc size without proportional value

**Output in this case:**
```markdown
## Improvement Suggestions

No significant improvements needed. Minor notes:
- [Low] {brief mention}
- [Medium] {brief mention}

These do not warrant doc changes due to low impact-to-size ratio.
```

**Anti-pattern: Doc bloat**
- Do NOT suggest adding sections that marginally improve clarity
- Do NOT suggest examples for obvious behavior
- Prefer concise docs over comprehensive but large ones

## Rules

**DO:**

- Read the entire item before judging
- Read other agents IN THE SAME SCOPE to check for conflicts
- Be specific about issues
- Provide actionable suggestions
- Use WARN for minor issues, FAIL for critical ones

**DON'T:**

- Auto-fix issues (report only)
- Validate multiple items at once
- Make changes to files
- Skip conflict detection
- Guess about unclear items — mark as WARN
- Recommend creating file-based versions of system agents
- Compare project/global agents with system agents as if they should match
- Reference files from lower scope than the item being validated

## Status Definitions

- **PASS** — Item meets all quality standards
- **WARN** — Minor issues found, item is usable
- **FAIL** — Critical issues found, item needs revision

## Examples

### Example: Good Agent (PASS)

```markdown
# Validation Report: developer

**Status:** PASS

## Summary

Well-defined developer agent with clear responsibilities and proper tool access.

## Checks

| Check                | Status | Notes                                     |
| -------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| Description Clarity  | PASS   | Clear trigger: "based on BA requirements" |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | Focused on implementation only            |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | No overlaps found                         |

| ...
```

### Example: Problematic Agent (FAIL)

```markdown
# Validation Report: helper

**Status:** FAIL

## Summary

Vague description and overlapping responsibilities with 3 other agents.

## Issues Found

### Issue 1: Vague description

**Severity:** Critical
**Location:** description field
**Problem:** "Helps with various tasks" - Claude won't know when to invoke
**Suggestion:** Specify exact trigger: "Formats markdown files after documentation updates"

### Issue 2: Overlapping with documenter

**Severity:** High
**Location:** Core Responsibilities section
**Problem:** "Updates documentation" - already done by documenter agent
**Suggestion:** Remove this responsibility or merge with documenter
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: agent-lint
description: Validates a single agent or command for quality, clarity, and conflicts. Use when auditing existing agents or before creating new ones.
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, WebSearch
model: sonnet
color: magenta
---

# Agent Lint

Validate agents and commands for quality and consistency.

## Agent Types

| Type        | Location           | Can Modify |
| ----------- | ------------------ | ---------- |
| **System**  | Built into Claude  | No         |
| **Global**  | `~/.claude/`       | Yes        |
| **Project** | `.claude/`         | Yes        |

**System agents** (built-in): `general-purpose`, `Explore`, `Plan`, `claude-code-guide`, `statusline-setup`
Never recommend creating file-based versions of system agents.

### Scope Hierarchy

When validating, only reference files at **same level or higher**:

- **Global agent** → compare with global + system only
- **Project agent** → compare with project + global + system

## Input

Receive ONE item: full path or name.

**Resolution** (if name only):

1. Search `.claude/agents/` and `.claude/commands/` (project)
2. Search `~/.claude/agents/` and `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
3. Use first match (project takes priority)

## What to Check

### 1. Description Clarity

Must be specific, unambiguous, actionable.
❌ "Helps with code" → ✅ "Reviews PR changes for security. Use after developer completes."

### 2. Responsibility Scope

- Focused (one clear purpose)
- Bounded (clear what it does NOT do)
- No overlap with other agents

### 3. Conflict Detection

Check same-scope agents for:

- Same trigger context
- Overlapping responsibility
- Contradicting instructions

### 4. Redundancy

- Duplicate functionality?
- Subset of another agent?
- Over-specialized (should merge)?

### 5. Tool Access (Agents)

- Minimal tools needed
- Write/Edit only if justified
- Consistent with role

### 6. Output Format

- Documented and consistent
- Machine-readable if needed

### 7. Instructions Quality

- Right altitude (not too rigid, not too vague)
- Has examples (good and bad)
- Covers main scenarios

## Optional: Web Research

If `--r` flag passed, search for similar agents and best practices online.

## Output Format

```markdown
# Validation Report: {name}

**Type:** Agent | Command
**File:** {path}
**Status:** PASS | WARN | FAIL

## Summary
{1-2 sentences}

## Checks

| Check                | Status | Notes |
| -------------------- | ------ | ----- |
| Description Clarity  | PASS   | ...   |
| Responsibility Scope | PASS   | ...   |
| Conflict Detection   | PASS   | ...   |
| Redundancy           | PASS   | ...   |
| Tool Access          | N/A    | ...   |
| Output Format        | PASS   | ...   |
| Instructions Quality | PASS   | ...   |

## Issues Found (if any)

### Issue: {title}
**Severity:** Critical | High | Medium | Low
**Location:** {section}
**Problem:** {what's wrong}
**Suggestion:** {how to fix}

## Improvement Suggestions

**Medium+ severity:** Full description with rationale
**Low severity:** Brief mention only

No improvements needed if only Low issues found.
```

## Severity & Actions

| Severity | Action                 |
| -------- | ---------------------- |
| Critical | MUST fix               |
| High     | MUST fix               |
| Medium   | Recommend fix          |
| Low      | Mention only           |

## Status Definitions

- **PASS** — meets all standards
- **WARN** — minor issues, usable
- **FAIL** — critical issues, needs revision

## Rules

- Read entire item before judging
- Read other agents IN SAME SCOPE for conflicts
- Be specific about issues
- Provide actionable suggestions
- Don't auto-fix (report only)
- Don't validate multiple items at once
- Don't compare project agents with system agents

content.text:
# Agent Updater

Apply improvements to agents, commands, or skills. Works in two modes:
1. **From recommendations** — apply changes saved by `/agent:improve`
2. **Direct update** — apply user's own improvement idea

**Use case:** Apply changes to agent files.
**Input:** Either saved recommendations or user's direct idea.

## Your Task

1. **Detect mode** — recommendations exist or direct update?
2. **Select scope** — global or project
3. **Identify target** — which file to update
4. **Show changes** — display exact diff before applying
5. **Validate syntax** — check YAML and markdown structure
6. **Create backup** — keep last 2 full copies
7. **Apply and log** — edit file, record to history

## Step 0: Detect Mode

Check if recommendations file exists:

```
{scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
```

**If exists and has recent content (today's date):**
- Read the file
- Show saved recommendations to user
- Ask which to apply

**If not exists or empty:**
- Proceed to direct update mode (Step 1)

### Recommendations Mode

When applying from suggestions.md:

1. Read the file
2. Parse selected recommendations
3. For each recommendation:
   - Show the exact change
   - Apply using Edit tool
4. After all applied, clear suggestions.md

## Step 0.5: Detect Rollback Request

Check if user input contains rollback keywords:

**Trigger keywords:** `rollback`, `revert`, `undo`, `restore`, `go back`

**If detected:**
1. Skip directly to Rollback Process (Step 8.5)
2. Ask which file to restore (if not specified)
3. Show available backups with modification dates
4. Confirm before restoring

**Example:**
```
User: /agent:update rollback developer

Claude: Found backups for developer.md:
- .bak1 (2025-12-05 14:30) — most recent
- .bak2 (2025-12-04 09:15) — previous

Which backup to restore?
```

## Step 1: Select Scope

**Auto-detect (by priority):**
1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`) → use that scope
2. Scope keyword ("global" or "project") → use that scope
3. File/agent name → search project first, then global
   - If file exists in **both** scopes, ask user which one to update
   - Never apply changes to both scopes simultaneously
4. Ambiguous → ask user

**Only if unclear**, use `AskUserQuestion`:
```json
{"questions": [{"question": "Which scope?", "header": "Scope", "options": [
  {"label": "Global", "description": "~/.claude/"},
  {"label": "Project", "description": ".claude/"}
], "multiSelect": false}]}
```

| Scope   | Base Path     | Use Case                        |
| ------- | ------------- | ------------------------------- |
| Global  | `~/.claude/`  | Personal settings, all projects |
| Project | `.claude/`    | Project-specific agents         |

**Remember selected scope** — use it for file paths, backups, and history.

## Step 2: Identify Target

User must specify what to update. If unclear, ask:

- "Which agent/command/skill do you want to update?"

Locations (relative to selected scope):

| Type    | Path                 |
| ------- | -------------------- |
| Agent   | `agents/{name}.md`   |
| Command | `commands/{name}.md` |
| Skill   | `skills/{name}/*.md` |

## Step 3: Clarify Change (Direct Mode Only)

For direct updates, ask if not clear:

- "What specific behavior should change?"
- "Why do you think this will improve the agent?"
- "Is this adding, modifying, or removing something?"

**Do not proceed until you understand the exact change.**

### Thinking Mode for Complex Changes

For changes that involve significant complexity, consider using thinking mode prompts:

| Complexity Signal | Recommended Approach |
| ----------------- | -------------------- |
| Architectural patterns | "Think step by step about the implications before proposing" |
| Multi-file coordination | "Analyze dependencies between affected files" |
| Backward compatibility | "Consider what existing workflows might break" |
| Performance-sensitive | "Evaluate potential performance tradeoffs" |

**When to trigger:** If the change touches core behavior, affects multiple sections, or the user expresses uncertainty about the approach.

### Web Research for Complex Changes

For complex or unprecedented changes, research best practices before proposing:

| Scenario | Search Query Example |
| -------- | -------------------- |
| Adding new tool to agent | "Claude Code agent tool permissions best practices 2025" |
| Changing agent description | "Claude Code agent description patterns invocation" |
| Multi-agent coordination | "Claude Code subagent orchestration patterns" |
| Performance optimization | "Claude Code agent model selection haiku vs sonnet" |
| New architectural pattern | "Claude Code agent design patterns" |
| Unfamiliar domain | "Claude Code [domain] conventions" |
| User unsure about approach | "Claude Code community examples [topic]" |

Use `WebSearch` with current year to find relevant patterns. This ensures updates align with established best practices rather than inventing new patterns.

## Step 4: Read Current File

Read the full target file. Note:

- File structure and sections
- Existing rules (avoid conflicts)
- YAML frontmatter format

## Step 5: Propose Change

**IMPORTANT:** Show ALL changes in detail before asking for confirmation.

For each change, show clearly:

```
## Proposed Update

**File:** [path]
**Section:** [section name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove

### Current:
[existing text, if modifying]

### After:
[new text]

**Rationale:** [why this improves behavior]
```

## Step 5.5: Ask Confirmation

Calculate and show change statistics:

```
Lines: +X / -Y (net: ±Z)
```

Use `AskUserQuestion` for confirmation:
```json
{"questions": [{"question": "Apply this change? (+X / -Y lines)", "header": "Confirm", "options": [
  {"label": "Yes", "description": "Apply and backup"},
  {"label": "Edit first", "description": "Modify proposal"},
  {"label": "No", "description": "Cancel"}
], "multiSelect": false}]}
```

## Step 6: Validate Syntax

Before applying, check:

**For agents (YAML frontmatter):**

- Valid YAML between `---` markers
- Required fields: `name`, `description`
- Optional: `tools`, `model`, `color`

**For commands:**

- Valid use of `agent:improve add Context Management and FORBIDDEN tools sections from backup` placeholder
- No conflicting argument patterns
- Description present (first non-empty line or frontmatter)

**For all files:**

- Markdown headings hierarchy valid
- No broken code blocks
- No duplicate sections

**If validation fails:**

1. Attempt automatic fix and show corrected version
2. If auto-fix not possible:
   - Show specific validation errors to user
   - Ask: "Fix manually and retry?" or "Cancel update?"
   - Do NOT apply invalid changes under any circumstances

## Step 7: Create Backup

On user confirmation:

1. **Check and create backup directory** in selected scope:

   ```
   {scope}/agents/.backups/    # for agents
   {scope}/commands/.backups/  # for commands
   {scope}/skills/.backups/    # for skills
   ```

   Pre-flight checks:
   - Verify directory exists, create if missing (`mkdir -p`)
   - Verify write access before proceeding
   - If cannot create or write, abort with error message

2. Save full file copy with rotation (keep last 2):

   - Delete `{name}.bak2` if exists
   - Move `{name}.bak1` → `{name}.bak2`
   - Copy **entire current file** → `{name}.bak1`

   Example: `~/.claude/agents/.backups/developer.md.bak1`

## Step 8: Apply and Log

1. Apply change using Edit tool

2. Log to history in selected scope:

   ```
   {scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
   ```

   Format:

   ```markdown
   ## [date] - [file name]

   **Source:** /agent:improve | direct update
   **Change:** [what was added/modified]
   **Status:** Applied
   ```

3. If applied from recommendations, clear suggestions.md:
   ```markdown
   # Suggestions

   No pending recommendations.
   ```

4. Report:

   ```
   Done: [file path]
   Backup: [backup path]
   Logged: {scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
   ```

## Rollback Process

If an update causes issues and needs to be reverted:

1. **Locate backup file:**
   ```
   {scope}/agents/.backups/{name}.md.bak1    # most recent
   {scope}/agents/.backups/{name}.md.bak2    # previous
   ```

2. **Restore from backup:**
   - Read backup file content
   - Write to original file location
   - Verify restoration succeeded

3. **Log rollback:**
   ```markdown
   ## [date] - [file name]

   **Source:** Rollback
   **Change:** Reverted to backup from [backup date]
   **Reason:** [user-provided reason]
   **Status:** Rolled back
   ```

4. **Report:**
   ```
   Rolled back: [file path]
   Restored from: [backup path]
   Logged: {scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
   ```

## Migration Between Scopes

When moving files between global and project scope:

1. **Create file in target scope** — write the updated content
2. **Verify new file** — confirm it was created correctly
3. **Delete old file** — remove from source scope
4. **Update backups** — store backup in target scope

**Never leave duplicate files** in both scopes after migration.

## Rules

- **Ask if unclear** — never guess user intent
- **Show all changes** — display complete diff before applying
- **Minimal changes** — only what's requested
- **Clean up after migration** — delete old files when moving between scopes
- **Validate syntax** — check before applying
- **Always backup** — before any edit
- **Always log** — for audit trail
- **Clear suggestions after apply** — prevent duplicate applications

## Change Size Guidelines

| Size   | Description              | Action                             |
| ------ | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| Small  | Fix typo, add one line   | Show diff, apply on confirm        |
| Medium | Add section, modify rule | Show diff, explain impact, confirm |
| Large  | Restructure, rewrite     | Suggest `/agent:create` instead    |

## Example: From Recommendations

```
User: /agent:update

Claude: Found recommendations from /agent:improve:

### 1. Add Loading States Checklist
**Section:** UI Standards
**Change:** [shows text]

Claude: [Uses AskUserQuestion: "Apply this?"]

User: [Selects "Yes"]

Claude:
Done: ~/.claude/agents/developer.md
Backup: ~/.claude/agents/.backups/developer.md.bak1
Cleared: suggestions.md
```

## Example: Direct Update

```
User: /agent:update add accessibility checklist to developer

Claude:
## Proposed Update

**File:** ~/.claude/agents/developer.md
**Section:** Code Standards
**Type:** Add

### After:
```markdown
### Accessibility
- Add ARIA labels to interactive elements
- Ensure keyboard navigation works
- Check color contrast ratios
```

Lines: +5 / -0

Claude: [Uses AskUserQuestion: "Apply this change?"]

User: [Selects "Yes"]

Claude:
Done: ~/.claude/agents/developer.md
Backup: ~/.claude/agents/.backups/developer.md.bak1
```

## Anti-Patterns

| Anti-Pattern           | Better Approach              |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Rewrite whole file     | Edit specific section only   |
| Skip validation        | Always check syntax first    |
| Skip backup            | Always backup before edit    |
| Guess user intent      | Ask for clarification        |
| Apply without showing  | Always show diff first       |
| Leave old suggestions  | Clear after applying         |

# Agent Analyzer

Analyze what went wrong and generate improvement recommendations.
**Does not apply changes** — use `/agent:update` to apply.

## Workflow

1. Select scope (auto-detect or ask)
2. Check improvement history for duplicates
3. Find user corrections in conversation
4. If unclear — ask what went wrong
5. Map issue to responsible agent
6. Assess severity
7. Research best practices (for High+ severity)
8. Self-critique proposal
9. Output recommendations
10. Save to suggestions.md for `/agent:update`

## Step 1: Select Scope

Auto-detect by priority:

1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`)
2. Keyword ("global" or "project")
3. File name → search project first, then global
4. Ambiguous → use `AskUserQuestion`

## Step 2: Check History

Read `{scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md`:

- Similar issue addressed before?
- Recurring pattern (3+ times)? → may need systemic solution

Skip duplicates already in suggestions.md or recently applied.

## Step 3: Find User Corrections

Look for patterns in conversation:

- "This should be X, not Y"
- "Missing X", "Wrong X", "Forgot to X"
- Direct edits user requested after agent output
- User provided their own solution

## Step 4: Clarify if Unclear

Use `AskUserQuestion` with options: Wrong output / Missing step / Too verbose / Not interactive

## Step 5: Map Issue to Agent

| Issue Type            | Agent           |
| --------------------- | --------------- |
| UI/styling/responsive | Developer       |
| Missing functionality | Developer       |
| Wrong business logic  | BA or Developer |
| Missing tests         | Tester          |
| Missed edge cases     | Tester          |
| Unclear requirements  | BA              |
| Documentation         | Documenter      |

## Step 6: Assess Severity

| Severity | Description                  | Action                      |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Critical | Broken, security issue       | Immediate fix + checklist   |
| High     | Missing common pattern       | Add to checklist            |
| Medium   | Edge case missed             | Add to examples             |
| Low      | Style/preference             | Document only, no rule      |

Low severity may not warrant agent changes.

## Step 7: Research (High+ Severity)

Search for:

- Claude Code agent patterns
- Community solutions
- Best practices for this issue type

Skip for Low severity or well-known patterns.

## Step 8: Self-Critique

Before presenting:

- Root cause or just symptom?
- Could cause false positives?
- Minimal change needed?
- Conflicts with existing rules?

Test: would this have prevented the original issue?

## Step 9: Output Recommendations

```markdown
## Analysis

**Issue:** [what user had to correct]
**Severity:** [level]
**Root cause:** [why missed]
**Agent:** [name]
**File:** [path]

## Research Findings (if performed)
[Key insights from sources]

---

## Recommendations

### 1. [Title]
**Section:** [name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove
**Change:**
```markdown
[exact text]
```
**Rationale:** [why this prevents the issue]

### 2. [Title]
...
```

Use `AskUserQuestion`: Apply All / 1 only / 1 and 2 / None

## Step 10: Save & Report

Save to `{scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md`:

```markdown
## [date] - Recommendations for [agent]

**Issue:** [brief]
**Selected:** [user's choice]

[Copy selected recommendations]
```

Report: `Saved to suggestions.md. Run /agent:update to apply.`

## Guardrails

- Maximum 5 recommendations per session
- 3+ recurring issues → escalate to documentation review
- Prefer general mechanisms over specific fixes
- Never duplicate existing functionality

## Rules

- **Analysis only** — NEVER use Edit, Write, or file-modification tools
- **Delegate changes** — all modifications via `/agent:update`
- **Ask first if unclear**
- **Research for High+ severity only**
- Read full agent file before proposing
- Make recommendations minimal and focused

1→# Agent Analyzer
     2→
     3→Analyze what went wrong and generate improvement recommendations. **Does not apply changes** — use `/agent:update` to apply.
     4→
     5→**Use case:** Something failed or worked poorly — analyze why and get recommendations.
     6→**Output:** List of recommendations for user to choose from.
     7→
     8→## Dialog Style
     9→
    10→- Use `AskUserQuestion` for all user interactions
    11→- Auto-detect scope/file when mentioned in conversation
    12→- Keep responses short — show details only when asked
    13→
    14→## Your Task
    15→
    16→1. **Select scope** — auto-detect or ask
    17→2. **Check improvement history** — silently, mention only if relevant
    18→3. **Review conversation history** — find where user requested fixes or pointed out issues
    19→4. **If unclear — ask user** — what specifically didn't work
    20→5. **Identify the pattern** — what type of issue was it
    21→6. **Assess severity** — is this worth a rule change?
    22→7. **Determine responsible agent** — which agent should have caught this
    23→8. **Research best practices** — search web for proven solutions
    24→9. **Self-critique proposal** — validate before presenting
    25→10. **Output recommendations** — user chooses what to apply via `/agent:update`
    26→
    27→## Analysis Steps
    28→
    29→### Step 0: Select Scope
    30→
    31→**Auto-detect (by priority):**
    32→1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`) → use that scope
    33→2. Scope keyword ("global" or "project") → use that scope
    34→3. File/agent name → search project first, then global
    35→4. Ambiguous → ask user
    36→
    37→**Edge case:** If file exists in BOTH scopes, ask which one to analyze.
    38→
    39→**Only if unclear**, use `AskUserQuestion`:
    40→```json
    41→{"questions": [{"question": "Which scope?", "header": "Scope", "options": [
    42→  {"label": "Global", "description": "~/.claude/"},
    43→  {"label": "Project", "description": ".claude/"}
    44→], "multiSelect": false}]}
    45→```
    46→
    47→### Step 1: Check Improvement History
    48→
    49→Check if improvement history exists in selected scope:
    50→
    51→```
    52→{scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
    53→```
    54→
    55→- Has similar issue been addressed before?
    56→- Did previous fix work or need revision?
    57→- Is this a recurring pattern?
    58→
    59→If recurring (3+ times), note this — may need systemic solution.
    60→
    61→### Step 1.5: Check for Duplicate Recommendations
    62→
    63→Before generating new recommendations:
    64→
    65→1. Read improvement history:
    66→   ```
    67→   {scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
    68→   ```
    69→
    70→2. Filter out issues that:
    71→   - Match previously applied changes (Status: Applied)
    72→   - Are currently pending in suggestions.md
    73→   - Address same root cause as recent fixes
    74→
    75→3. If issue was already addressed:
    76→   - Show user: "This issue was addressed on [date] with [change]"
    77→   - Ask: "Re-analyze anyway? [Yes/No]"
    78→
    79→### Step 2: Find User Corrections
    80→
    81→Look for patterns in conversation:
    82→
    83→- "This should be X, not Y"
    84→- "Missing X"
    85→- "Wrong X"
    86→- "Forgot to X"
    87→- "X doesn't work"
    88→- Direct edits user requested after agent output
    89→- **User provided their own solution** — proposal was insufficient
    90→
    91→### Step 2.5: Context Management
    92→
    93→For long conversation histories (100+ messages):
    94→
    95→- Summarize older messages into key decisions
    96→- Retain only recent 20-30 messages in full detail
    97→- Focus on user corrections and explicit feedback
    98→
    99→### Step 3: If Unclear — Ask User
   100→
   101→If problem is not clear, use `AskUserQuestion`:
   102→```json
   103→{"questions": [{"question": "What went wrong?", "header": "Problem", "options": [
   104→  {"label": "Wrong output", "description": "Incorrect result"},
   105→  {"label": "Missing step", "description": "Forgot something"},
   106→  {"label": "Too verbose", "description": "Too much text"},
   107→  {"label": "Not interactive", "description": "Should ask questions"}
   108→], "multiSelect": true}]}
   109→```
   110→
   111→**Do not proceed until clear.**
   112→
   113→### Step 4: Map Issue to Agent
   114→
   115→| Issue Type            | Primary Agent   | File                        |
   116→| --------------------- | --------------- | --------------------------- |
   117→| UI/styling            | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
   118→| Missing functionality | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
   119→| Wrong business logic  | BA or Developer | `agents/business-analyst.md`|
   120→| Missing tests         | Tester          | `agents/tester.md`          |
   121→| Missed edge cases     | Tester          | `agents/tester.md`          |
   122→| Accessibility         | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
   123→| Responsive/mobile     | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
   124→| Documentation         | Documenter      | `agents/documenter.md`      |
   125→| Unclear requirements  | BA              | `agents/business-analyst.md`|
   126→
   127→### Step 4.5: Assess Severity
   128→
   129→| Severity | Description                          | Action                                   |
   130→| -------- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
   131→| Critical | Broken functionality, security issue | Immediate fix + mandatory checklist item |
   132→| High     | Missing common pattern               | Add to checklist                         |
   133→| Medium   | Edge case missed                     | Add to examples section                  |
   134→| Low      | Style/preference                     | Document in notes, don't add rule        |
   135→
   136→Low severity issues may not warrant agent changes.
   137→
   138→### Step 5: Read Agent File
   139→
   140→Read the identified agent file completely. Find:
   141→
   142→- Missing rules
   143→- Incomplete checklists
   144→- Missing examples
   145→- Vague instructions
   146→
   147→### Step 6: Research Best Practices
   148→
   149→**When to research:**
   150→- First time seeing this issue type
   151→- Issue severity is High or Critical
   152→- User explicitly requested research
   153→- No clear pattern in conversation history
   154→
   155→**When to skip:**
   156→- Low severity issues
   157→- Well-known patterns (missing tests, typos)
   158→- Time-sensitive fixes
   159→- Issue already has clear solution in conversation
   160→
   161→**Search the web for:**
   162→
   163→- How other Claude Code agents handle this issue
   164→- Best practices for AI agent instructions
   165→- Community solutions in `awesome-claude-code` repositories
   166→
   167→Search queries:
   168→
   169→- `"Claude Code agent" [issue type] best practices`
   170→- `"AI coding agent" [issue type] instructions`
   171→- `claude agent prompt [issue type]`
   172→
   173→**Extract actionable patterns** from research.
   174→
   175→### Step 7: Self-Critique
   176→

# Agent Analyzer

Analyze what went wrong and generate improvement recommendations. **Does not apply changes** — use `/agent:update` to apply.

**Use case:** Something failed or worked poorly — analyze why and get recommendations.
**Output:** List of recommendations for user to choose from.

## Dialog Style

- Use `AskUserQuestion` for all user interactions
- Auto-detect scope/file when mentioned in conversation
- Keep responses short — show details only when asked

## Your Task

1. **Select scope** — auto-detect or ask
2. **Check improvement history** — silently, mention only if relevant
3. **Review conversation history** — find where user requested fixes or pointed out issues
4. **If unclear — ask user** — what specifically didn't work
5. **Identify the pattern** — what type of issue was it
6. **Assess severity** — is this worth a rule change?
7. **Determine responsible agent** — which agent should have caught this
8. **Research best practices** — search web for proven solutions
9. **Self-critique proposal** — validate before presenting
10. **Output recommendations** — user chooses what to apply via `/agent:update`

## Analysis Steps

### Step 0: Select Scope

**Auto-detect (by priority):**
1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`) → use that scope
2. Scope keyword ("global" or "project") → use that scope
3. File/agent name → search project first, then global
4. Ambiguous → ask user

**Edge case:** If file exists in BOTH scopes, ask which one to analyze.

**Only if unclear**, use `AskUserQuestion`:
```json
{"questions": [{"question": "Which scope?", "header": "Scope", "options": [
  {"label": "Global", "description": "~/.claude/"},
  {"label": "Project", "description": ".claude/"}
], "multiSelect": false}]}
```

### Step 1: Check Improvement History

Check if improvement history exists in selected scope:

```
{scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
```

- Has similar issue been addressed before?
- Did previous fix work or need revision?
- Is this a recurring pattern?

If recurring (3+ times), note this — may need systemic solution.

### Step 1.5: Check for Duplicate Recommendations

Before generating new recommendations:

1. Read improvement history:
   ```
   {scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md
   ```

2. Filter out issues that:
   - Match previously applied changes (Status: Applied)
   - Are currently pending in suggestions.md
   - Address same root cause as recent fixes

3. If issue was already addressed:
   - Show user: "This issue was addressed on [date] with [change]"
   - Ask: "Re-analyze anyway? [Yes/No]"

### Step 2: Find User Corrections

Look for patterns in conversation:

- "This should be X, not Y"
- "Missing X"
- "Wrong X"
- "Forgot to X"
- "X doesn't work"
- Direct edits user requested after agent output
- **User provided their own solution** — proposal was insufficient

### Step 2.5: Context Management

For long conversation histories (100+ messages):

- Summarize older messages into key decisions
- Retain only recent 20-30 messages in full detail
- Focus on user corrections and explicit feedback

### Step 3: If Unclear — Ask User

If problem is not clear, use `AskUserQuestion`:
```json
{"questions": [{"question": "What went wrong?", "header": "Problem", "options": [
  {"label": "Wrong output", "description": "Incorrect result"},
  {"label": "Missing step", "description": "Forgot something"},
  {"label": "Too verbose", "description": "Too much text"},
  {"label": "Not interactive", "description": "Should ask questions"}
], "multiSelect": true}]}
```

**Do not proceed until clear.**

### Step 4: Map Issue to Agent

| Issue Type            | Primary Agent   | File                        |
| --------------------- | --------------- | --------------------------- |
| UI/styling            | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
| Missing functionality | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
| Wrong business logic  | BA or Developer | `agents/business-analyst.md`|
| Missing tests         | Tester          | `agents/tester.md`          |
| Missed edge cases     | Tester          | `agents/tester.md`          |
| Accessibility         | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
| Responsive/mobile     | Developer       | `agents/developer.md`       |
| Documentation         | Documenter      | `agents/documenter.md`      |
| Unclear requirements  | BA              | `agents/business-analyst.md`|

### Step 4.5: Assess Severity

| Severity | Description                          | Action                                   |
| -------- | ------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------- |
| Critical | Broken functionality, security issue | Immediate fix + mandatory checklist item |
| High     | Missing common pattern               | Add to checklist                         |
| Medium   | Edge case missed                     | Add to examples section                  |
| Low      | Style/preference                     | Document in notes, don't add rule        |

Low severity issues may not warrant agent changes.

### Step 5: Read Agent File

Read the identified agent file completely. Find:

- Missing rules
- Incomplete checklists
- Missing examples
- Vague instructions

### Step 6: Research Best Practices

**When to research:**
- First time seeing this issue type
- Issue severity is High or Critical
- User explicitly requested research
- No clear pattern in conversation history

**When to skip:**
- Low severity issues
- Well-known patterns (missing tests, typos)
- Time-sensitive fixes
- Issue already has clear solution in conversation

**Search the web for:**

- How other Claude Code agents handle this issue
- Best practices for AI agent instructions
- Community solutions in `awesome-claude-code` repositories

Search queries:

- `"Claude Code agent" [issue type] best practices`
- `"AI coding agent" [issue type] instructions`
- `claude agent prompt [issue type]`

**Extract actionable patterns** from research.

### Step 7: Self-Critique

Before presenting, critique your own proposals:

**Conceptual checks:**
- Does this solve the root cause or just the symptom?
- Could this rule cause false positives?
- Is this the minimal change needed?
- Will this conflict with existing rules?
- Is this generalizable or too specific?

**Empirical validation:**
- Would this rule have prevented the original issue?
- Test against 2-3 similar scenarios from conversation history

If concerns found, refine before presenting.

### Step 8: Output Recommendations

**Format recommendations as a numbered list for user to choose from:**

```
## Analysis

**Issue found:** [what user had to correct]
**Severity:** [Critical/High/Medium/Low]
**Root cause:** [why agent missed this]
**Agent to improve:** [agent name]
**File:** [path]

## Research Findings

**Sources checked:**
- [source 1]: [key insight]
- [source 2]: [key insight]

**Best practice identified:** [what works well]

---

## Recommendations

Select which recommendations to apply with `/agent:update`:

### 1. [Short title]
**Section:** [section name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove
**Change:**
```markdown
[exact text to add/change]
```
**Rationale:** [why this prevents the issue]

### 2. [Short title]
**Section:** [section name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove
**Change:**
```markdown
[exact text to add/change]
```
**Rationale:** [why this helps]

### 3. [Short title]
...
```

### Step 9: Ask User to Choose

Use `AskUserQuestion`:
```json
{"questions": [{"question": "Which recommendations to apply?", "header": "Apply", "options": [
  {"label": "All", "description": "Apply all recommendations"},
  {"label": "1 only", "description": "[title of rec 1]"},
  {"label": "1 and 2", "description": "First two recommendations"},
  {"label": "None", "description": "Skip for now"}
], "multiSelect": false}]}
```

### Step 10: Save Recommendations

Save recommendations to file for `/agent:update` to consume:

```
{scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
```

Format:
```markdown
## [date] - Recommendations for [agent name]

**Issue:** [brief description]
**Generated by:** /agent:improve

### Selected: [user's choice]

[Copy selected recommendations here with full details]
```

Then inform user:
```
Recommendations saved to {scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md

To apply, run: /agent:update
```

## Guardrails

- Maximum 5 recommendations per session
- If same issue appears 3+ times after fixes, escalate to documentation review
- Avoid "rule for every edge case" — look for generalizable patterns
- Never add rules that duplicate existing functionality
- **Prefer general mechanisms over specific fixes**

## Rules

- **Analysis only** — NEVER use Edit, Write, or any file-modification tools
- **Delegate changes** — all modifications go through `/agent:update`
- **Ask first if unclear** — don't guess what user wants
- **Research for High+ severity** — skip for low severity and well-known patterns
- Read full agent file before proposing changes
- Make recommendations minimal and focused
- Save recommendations for `/agent:update` to apply

**FORBIDDEN tools in this command:**
- Edit
- Write
- NotebookEdit
- Any tool that modifies files

If you catch yourself about to use Edit/Write — STOP and save to suggestions.md instead.

## Example Flow

```
User: /agent:improve

Claude: [Uses AskUserQuestion: "What went wrong?"]

User: [Selects "Missing step"]

Claude: [Uses AskUserQuestion: "Which agent?"]

User: [Selects "Developer"]

Claude:
## Analysis
**Issue found:** Missing loading states
**Severity:** High
**Agent:** Developer

## Recommendations

### 1. Add Loading States Checklist
**Section:** UI Standards
**Change:** [shows exact text]

### 2. Add Example
**Section:** Examples
**Change:** [shows example]

Claude: [Uses AskUserQuestion: "Which to apply?"]

User: [Selects "1 only"]

Claude:
Saved to ~/.claude/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md
To apply, run: /agent:update
```

# Agent Analyzer

Analyze what went wrong and generate improvement recommendations.
**Does not apply changes** — use `/agent:update` to apply.

## Workflow

1. Select scope (auto-detect or ask)
2. Check improvement history for duplicates
3. Find user corrections in conversation
4. If unclear — ask what went wrong
5. Map issue to responsible agent
6. Assess severity
7. Research best practices (for High+ severity)
8. Self-critique proposal
9. Output recommendations
10. Save to suggestions.md for `/agent:update`

## Step 1: Select Scope

Auto-detect by priority:

1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`)
2. Keyword ("global" or "project")
3. File name → search project first, then global
4. Ambiguous → use `AskUserQuestion`

## Step 2: Check History

Read `{scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md`:

- Similar issue addressed before?
- Recurring pattern (3+ times)? → may need systemic solution

Skip duplicates already in suggestions.md or recently applied.

## Step 3: Find User Corrections

Look for patterns in conversation:

- "This should be X, not Y"
- "Missing X", "Wrong X", "Forgot to X"
- Direct edits user requested after agent output
- User provided their own solution

## Step 4: Clarify if Unclear

Use `AskUserQuestion` with options: Wrong output / Missing step / Too verbose / Not interactive

## Step 5: Map Issue to Agent

| Issue Type            | Agent           |
| --------------------- | --------------- |
| UI/styling/responsive | Developer       |
| Missing functionality | Developer       |
| Wrong business logic  | BA or Developer |
| Missing tests         | Tester          |
| Missed edge cases     | Tester          |
| Unclear requirements  | BA              |
| Documentation         | Documenter      |

## Step 6: Assess Severity

| Severity | Description                  | Action                      |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Critical | Broken, security issue       | Immediate fix + checklist   |
| High     | Missing common pattern       | Add to checklist            |
| Medium   | Edge case missed             | Add to examples             |
| Low      | Style/preference             | Document only, no rule      |

Low severity may not warrant agent changes.

## Step 7: Research (High+ Severity)

Search for:

- Claude Code agent patterns
- Community solutions
- Best practices for this issue type

Skip for Low severity or well-known patterns.

## Step 8: Self-Critique

Before presenting:

- Root cause or just symptom?
- Could cause false positives?
- Minimal change needed?
- Conflicts with existing rules?

Test: would this have prevented the original issue?

## Step 9: Output Recommendations

```markdown
## Analysis

**Issue:** [what user had to correct]
**Severity:** [level]
**Root cause:** [why missed]
**Agent:** [name]
**File:** [path]

## Research Findings (if performed)
[Key insights from sources]

---

## Recommendations

### 1. [Title]
**Section:** [name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove
**Change:**
```markdown
[exact text]
```
**Rationale:** [why this prevents the issue]

### 2. [Title]
...
```

Use `AskUserQuestion`: Apply All / 1 only / 1 and 2 / None

## Step 10: Save & Report

Save to `{scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md`:

```markdown
## [date] - Recommendations for [agent]

**Issue:** [brief]
**Selected:** [user's choice]

[Copy selected recommendations]
```

Report: `Saved to suggestions.md. Run /agent:update to apply.`

## Guardrails

- Maximum 5 recommendations per session
- 3+ recurring issues → escalate to documentation review
- Prefer general mechanisms over specific fixes
- Never duplicate existing functionality

## Rules

- **Analysis only** — NEVER use Edit, Write, or file-modification tools
- **Delegate changes** — all modifications via `/agent:update`
- **Ask first if unclear**
- **Research for High+ severity only**
- Read full agent file before proposing
- Make recommendations minimal and focused

# Agent Analyzer

Analyze what went wrong and generate improvement recommendations.
**Does not apply changes** — use `/agent:update` to apply.

## Workflow

1. Select scope (auto-detect or ask)
2. Check improvement history for duplicates
3. Find user corrections in conversation
4. If unclear — ask what went wrong
5. Map issue to responsible agent
6. Assess severity
7. Research best practices (for High+ severity)
8. Self-critique proposal
9. Output recommendations
10. Save to suggestions.md for `/agent:update`

## Step 1: Select Scope

Auto-detect by priority:

1. Explicit path (`~/.claude/` or `.claude/`)
2. Keyword ("global" or "project")
3. File name → search project first, then global
4. Ambiguous → use `AskUserQuestion`

## Step 2: Check History

Read `{scope}/agents/.improvements/history.md`:

- Similar issue addressed before?
- Recurring pattern (3+ times)? → may need systemic solution

Skip duplicates already in suggestions.md or recently applied.

## Step 3: Find User Corrections

Look for patterns in conversation:

- "This should be X, not Y"
- "Missing X", "Wrong X", "Forgot to X"
- Direct edits user requested after agent output
- User provided their own solution

## Step 3.5: Context Management

For long conversation histories (100+ messages):

- Summarize older messages into key decisions
- Retain only recent 20-30 messages in full detail
- Focus on user corrections and explicit feedback

## Step 4: Clarify if Unclear

Use `AskUserQuestion` with options: Wrong output / Missing step / Too verbose / Not interactive

## Step 5: Map Issue to Agent

| Issue Type            | Agent           |
| --------------------- | --------------- |
| UI/styling/responsive | Developer       |
| Missing functionality | Developer       |
| Wrong business logic  | BA or Developer |
| Missing tests         | Tester          |
| Missed edge cases     | Tester          |
| Unclear requirements  | BA              |
| Documentation         | Documenter      |

## Step 6: Assess Severity

| Severity | Description                  | Action                      |
| -------- | ---------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Critical | Broken, security issue       | Immediate fix + checklist   |
| High     | Missing common pattern       | Add to checklist            |
| Medium   | Edge case missed             | Add to examples             |
| Low      | Style/preference             | Document only, no rule      |

Low severity may not warrant agent changes.

## Step 7: Research (High+ Severity)

Search for:

- Claude Code agent patterns
- Community solutions
- Best practices for this issue type

Skip for Low severity or well-known patterns.

## Step 8: Self-Critique

Before presenting:

- Root cause or just symptom?
- Could cause false positives?
- Minimal change needed?
- Conflicts with existing rules?

Test: would this have prevented the original issue?

## Step 9: Output Recommendations

```markdown
## Analysis

**Issue:** [what user had to correct]
**Severity:** [level]
**Root cause:** [why missed]
**Agent:** [name]
**File:** [path]

## Research Findings (if performed)
[Key insights from sources]

---

## Recommendations

### 1. [Title]
**Section:** [name]
**Type:** Add | Modify | Remove
**Change:**
```markdown
[exact text]
```
**Rationale:** [why this prevents the issue]

### 2. [Title]
...
```

Use `AskUserQuestion`: Apply All / 1 only / 1 and 2 / None

## Step 10: Save & Report

Save to `{scope}/agents/.improvements/suggestions.md`:

```markdown
## [date] - Recommendations for [agent]

**Issue:** [brief]
**Selected:** [user's choice]

[Copy selected recommendations]
```

Report: `Saved to suggestions.md. Run /agent:update to apply.`

## Guardrails

- Maximum 5 recommendations per session
- 3+ recurring issues → escalate to documentation review
- Prefer general mechanisms over specific fixes
- Never duplicate existing functionality

## Rules

- **Analysis only** — NEVER use Edit, Write, or file-modification tools
- **Delegate changes** — all modifications via `/agent:update`
- **Ask first if unclear**
- **Research for High+ severity only**
- Read full agent file before proposing
- Make recommendations minimal and focused
